title: Clean Lands
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:Honoghr1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]], showing the relative size and location of the Clean Lands.]]
The '''Clean Lands''' was the term given by the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] to a region of decontaminated soil on the surface of their homeworld of [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]], restored to fertility by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Decon III|decontamination]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]] some time after that [[Planet/Legends|world]] was [[Battle of Honoghr/Legends|devastated]] by the chemical weapon [[Trihexalophine1138]]. The region was centered around the capital of [[Nystao]], and encompassed the territories of [[Bahk'tor]], [[Zhuk'nir]], [[Kihm'bar]], [[Lohk'nar]], [[Hakh'khar]], and [[Elkh'mir]].

==Origins==
During the height of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] a space battle erupted between the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] and forces of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems's]] [[Confederate Navy/Legends|navy]] in orbit over Honoghr. In the wake of the battle a damaged [[Core ship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class Core Ship]] crashed into the planet's surface, releasing copious amounts of [[Trihexalophine1138]], a deadly toxin that the CIS had developed for use in chemical warfare against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. The chemical proceeded to poison great swaths of Honoghr, killing many millions of Noghri in addition to countless native animals and plant life. At the same time, stray fire from both sides rained destruction down upon the surface, making matters worse.

[[File:CleanLands-TLC2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The river valley clean land]]
After the establishment of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] and near the beginning of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the planet and its people were "rediscovered" by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Impressed with the combat capabilities of the native Noghri, who had slaughtered Vader's [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] escort, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] engineered a bargain by which the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] would use its resources to "help" the natives reclaim their land—the desecration of which Vader conveniently blamed on the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]—in exchange for their pledge of servitude. Incapable of understanding the complexities of toxic materials and the cleaning of same, the Noghri were unable to comprehend, at first, that the help given by the Imperials was actually in the form of a self-perpetuating cycle that would guarantee eternal service in the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] private [[Noghri Death Commando]]s.

==Aftermath==
When [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] [[Mission to Honoghr|journeyed]] to Honoghr in an attempt to understand the Noghri and persuade them to abandon the Empire, she observed the cleansing process, eventually determining, with the aid of [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], exactly how the decon [[Droid/Legends|droids]] worked. Once the secret of Imperial treachery was exposed to the assembled Clan [[Dynast]]s at the capital of [[Nystao]], the Noghri [[Noghri revolution|swore]] to avenge themselves upon the Empire that had deceived them. Some time later, several Noghri clans used the same decontaminating technology, now having learned how to use it properly, to restore another patch of clean land on another part of Honoghr. This new area of restored land was located in a narrow river valley in order to keep it hidden from orbiting Imperial [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. It was here that [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] first met the Noghri.
